mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are positive in >65% of rate hike periods.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration that reduces consumer spending expectations, driving negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that supports corporate earnings growth, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >55% of co-occurring expansion periods, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion reflects capacity constraints and weakening manufacturing activity, triggering short-term equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are positive in >60% of late-cycle periods when INDPRO declines, or the mechanism fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, borrowing costs severely constrain corporate leverage and capex plans, producing negative forward equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of observations when FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0 percent, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total employment and falling unemployment rate indicates labor market tightening that fuels wage inflation and margin compression, leading to negative quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rebound in housing starts during a post-shock recovery phase signals renewed consumer confidence and construction sector activity, driving positive short-term equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: Housing start rebounds fail to generate positive 5-day returns in >60% of post-shock recovery periods, or the signal shows no predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding economic output and higher corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in >60% of periods when INDPRO expands, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling evaluation windows.
